title: Nvidia Q3 FY2026 Financial Results
content: SANTA CLARA, Calif., November 20, 2025 — Nvidia today reported revenue for the third quarter ended October 27, 2025, of $35.1 billion, up 94% from a year ago and up 17% from the previous quarter. GAAP earnings per diluted share were $0.78, up 111% from a year ago. Non-GAAP earnings per diluted share were $0.81, up 103% from a year ago. Data Center revenue was $30.8 billion, up 112% year-on-year, reflecting shipments of H200 and Blackwell B200 GPUs to hyperscalers and enterprise customers building AI infrastructure. Gaming revenue was $3.3 billion, up 15%, driven by the RTX 50 series GPU launch. Professional Visualization was $0.5 billion, up 17%. Automotive revenue was $0.45 billion, up 72% as autonomous vehicle programmes scale. Gross margin was 74.6% GAAP and 75.0% non-GAAP. Jensen Huang, Nvidia's CEO, said in the November 20, 2025 earnings call: 'The scale-up and scale-out of AI infrastructure is continuing and accelerating. Blackwell is in full production and demand remains high.' Nvidia repurchased 69 million shares for $8.4 billion during the quarter and declared a $0.01 per share quarterly cash dividend. The company guided Q4 FY2026 revenue to $37.5 billion, plus or minus 2%. Nvidia's CUDA software platform has over 5 million registered developers. The company employs approximately 32,000 people globally and is headquartered in Santa Clara, California.
